steps.title: Understand the weekly decrease
steps.content:
Each week, the number of books decreases by 1/2 of the previous amount. This means: $\text{new amount} = \text{previous} - \frac{1}{2} \cdot \text{previous} = \frac{1}{2} \cdot \text{previous}$
So the collection is multiplied by $\frac{1}{2}$ each week.

steps.title: Build the exponential formula
steps.content:
Starting with 3,600 books, applying a factor of $\frac{1}{2}$ for each of $w$ weeks:
$$c = 3,600 \left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^w$$
Verify at $w = 1$: $c = 3,600 \cdot \frac{1}{2} = 1,800$ (half of 3,600, correct for one week).
That confirms D is correct.
